clean.label: Clean Host
clean.description: A normal workstation process tree with expected parent-child relationships.

nodes.name: explorer.exe
nodes.reason: The Windows shell, launched by userinit. Expected root of user processes.

nodes.name: chrome.exe
nodes.reason: Browser launched from the shell - normal.

nodes.name: outlook.exe
nodes.reason: Mail client launched from the shell - normal.

nodes.name: powershell.exe
nodes.reason: Interactive PowerShell running a routine read-only command, launched by the user from explorer.

nodes.name: cmd.exe
nodes.reason: User-launched command prompt - normal when parented by explorer.

phishing.label: Phishing -> Payload
phishing.description: A malicious Office macro chain: an email attachment spawns PowerShell, which loads a payload via rundll32.

nodes.name: outlook.exe
nodes.reason: The user opened Outlook and a phishing email - the app itself is legitimate.

nodes.name: winword.exe
nodes.reason: Word opened a macro-enabled attachment from Outlook. Legitimate on its own, but an Office app spawning children is a classic macro red flag.

nodes.name: powershell.exe
nodes.reason: WINWORD spawned a hidden, encoded PowerShell - a document macro executing a payload. Office should never spawn PowerShell.

nodes.name: rundll32.exe
nodes.reason: rundll32 launched with no DLL or arguments - a hallmark of injected code hiding behind a signed binary.

nodes.name: cmd.exe
nodes.reason: Post-exploitation discovery commands run by the payload.

nodes.name: whoami.exe
nodes.reason: Identity discovery - low signal alone, but part of the chain here.

ransomware.label: Ransomware Execution
ransomware.description: A user runs a double-extension executable that deletes backups and prepares to encrypt.

nodes.name: Invoice_2026.pdf.exe
nodes.reason: A double-extension executable run from Downloads - the ransomware dropper masquerading as a PDF.

nodes.name: vssadmin.exe
nodes.reason: Deletes Volume Shadow Copies to prevent recovery - a near-universal ransomware precursor.

nodes.name: wmic.exe
nodes.reason: A second method to delete shadow copies.

nodes.name: bcdedit.exe
nodes.reason: Disables Windows recovery - another anti-recovery step.

nodes.name: cmd.exe
nodes.reason: Stops processes that lock target files before encryption.
